Title: A novel R2R3-MYB transcription factor FaMYB5 positively regulates anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin biosynthesis in cultivated strawberries

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin accumulation indirectly by increasing FaMYB5 transcript levels.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; HPLC; DMACA assay; transcriptome co-location prediction
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaMYB5-overexpression fruits; two homologous transcripts identified from transcriptome data
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-transcriptome sequencing, CNSA: CNP0002209; metabolomics, accession NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on the antisense strand of FaMYB5; overexpression increased FaMYB5 transcription by ~70%; inferred to fine-tune FaMYB5 transcription and possibly form a self-activation route.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lncRNA-myb5 is an antisense lncRNA associated with FaMYB5. 2. Its overexpression increased FaMYB5 transcript abundance and upregulated PAL, C4H, CHS, LAR, F3'H and TT12. 3. It caused modest increases in pelargonidin 3-O-glucoside, cyanidin 3-O-glucoside and proanthocyanidins.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream noncoding target to fine-tune FaMYB5-driven flavonoid enhancement in strawberry.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious flesh pigmentation by appearance despite metabolite increases; mechanism remains unclear.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively affects anthocyanin/proanthocyanidin accumulation by promoting FaMYB5 protein degradation through the ubiquitin/26S proteasome pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; modified dual-luciferase protein degradation assay; in vitro degradation assay; in vitro ubiquitination assay
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Upregulated after FaMYB5 overexpression; homolog of apple MdBT2; mediates ubiquitination-associated turnover of FaMYB5.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaBT2 physically interacts with FaMYB5. 2. FaBT2 accelerates FaMYB5 degradation in vivo and in vitro. 3. MG132 suppresses this degradation, supporting ubiquitin/26S proteasome involvement.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for editing or suppression to stabilize FaMYB5 and enhance fruit flavonoids.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible broader proteostasis effects not characterized.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with FaMYB5 for anthocyanin recovery in myb10 mutant fruit, consistent with a transport/sequestration role.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression alone and co-overexpression with FaMYB5; HPLC; total anthocyanin assay; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Barely expressed in Snowwhite flesh; strongly induced in red fruit according to discussion/supporting data
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaMYB5 alone failed to restore stable anthocyanin accumulation in Snowwhite because it did not upregulate TT19; TT19 alone was insufficient, but co-overexpression with FaMYB5 partially restored anthocyanins.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT19 expression is limiting in myb10 mutant Snowwhite fruit. 2. FaMYB5 does not activate TT19, unlike FaMYB10. 3. Co-expression of TT19 with FaMYB5 partially rescues anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important co-engineering target with FaMYB5 for stable anthocyanin deposition, especially in backgrounds lacking TT19 expression.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: TT19 alone does not restore pigmentation; requires compatible biosynthetic activation background.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cyanidin/delphinidin-related hydroxylation branch; PA precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: flavanones/dihydroflavonols such as naringenin or dihydrokaempferol
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3'-hydroxylated flavonoids such as eriodictyol or dihydroquercetin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a 3'-hydroxyl group on the B-ring of flavonoid intermediates, redirecting flux toward cyanidin-type anthocyanins and related flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control step supported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cyanidin-type anthocyanin accumulation; its overexpression restored pigment accumulation and Cy3G production in strawberry fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FaMYB5 overexpression expression analysis; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; transient overexpression of FaF3'H; HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by FaMYB5 overexpression in fruits and transgenic diploid strawberry plants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient overexpression; native promoter tested in dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional target of FaMYB5; promoter contains MYB recognition site bound by FaMYB5.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaF3'H is one of the strongest induced structural genes after FaMYB5 overexpression. 2. FaMYB5 directly binds and activates the FaF3'H promoter. 3. Transient FaF3'H overexpression increases pigmentation and restores Cy3G accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering cyanidin enrichment and expanding anthocyanin diversity in strawberry.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux to cyanidin branch and alter metabolite composition beyond target pigments.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-transcriptome sequencing, CNSA: CNP0002209; targeted and quasi-targeted metabolomics, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum (typical for cytochrome P450; inferred)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces leucoanthocyanidins to catechin, contributing to proanthocyanidin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for PA branch supported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proanthocyanidin accumulation; strongly induced and directly activated by FaMYB5.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FaMYB5 overexpression expression analysis; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by FaMYB5 overexpression in fruits and diploid transgenic strawberry plants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for FaMYB5 overexpression; native LAR promoter tested in dual-luciferase
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional target of FaMYB5; promoter contains MYB recognition site bound by FaMYB5.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaLAR is among the most strongly upregulated genes after FaMYB5 overexpression. 2. FaMYB5 directly binds and activates the FaLAR promoter. 3. This direct regulation explains enhanced PA accumulation in FaMYB5-overexpressing fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for boosting PA content and flavan-3-ol biosynthesis, potentially in combination with FaMYB5/MBW components.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May channel flux from anthocyanin intermediates into PA branch, altering color/metabolite balance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-transcriptome sequencing, CNSA: CNP0002209; metabolomics, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Reductase of the SDR-like flavonoid enzyme class

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Xiaobai; Benihoppe; Snowwhite; diploid strawberry Ruegen
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contribute to fruit quality and pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor with conserved bHLH-interaction motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: flavonoid biosynthesis; PA biosynthesis; anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates F3'H and LAR directly; upregulates many flavonoid structural genes and transport-related genes indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin accumulation in cultivated strawberry fruits.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in fruits; HPLC; DMACA assay; targeted and quasi-targeted metabolomics; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; transgenic diploid plants
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive activator of both anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis, broader than FaMYB10 and effective across tissues/stages
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with FaEGL3 and FaLWD1/FaLWD1-like
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: lncRNA-myb5 positive transcriptional tuning; FaBT2-mediated proteasomal degradation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely self-activation/feedforward via induction of lncRNA-myb5 transcripts inferred by authors
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in multiple tissues; highest in fruit and increasing during fruit reddening
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In myb10 mutant Snowwhite, cannot stably restore anthocyanins without TT19; anthocyanin restoration near achenes can occur under low temperature, salt stress and disease contexts discussed for mutant background
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from previously annotated R3-FaMYB5; broader spatial/temporal role than fruit-specific FaMYB10; also influences phenylpropanoid and lignin pathway-related transcripts/metabolites.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB5 is a bona fide R2R3-MYB activator, not an R3-MYB repressor. 2. Overexpression increases anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins and broadly elevates flavonoid pathway gene expression. 3. It directly activates F3'H and LAR and functions with FaEGL3/FaLWD1(FaLWD1-like) in a new MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to co-enhance strawberry color and PA nutrition; may be stacked with TT19 and MBW partners for stronger/stable effects.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient alone in myb10 mutant fruit lacking TT19 expression; may also perturb lignin/phenylpropanoid metabolism and broader phenolic profiles.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-transcriptome sequencing, CNSA: CNP0002209; targeted and quasi-targeted metabolomics, accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid structural genes within FaMYB5 MBW output including F3'H, LAR, ANS, TT12, AHA10, PAL, C4H, CHS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances FaMYB5-mediated activation of flavonoid biosynthetic and transport genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin and especially PA accumulation together with FaMYB5 and WD40 partners.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-overexpression in fruits; HPLC; DMACA assay; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential bHLH partner in the newly defined FaMYB5-centered MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (inferred as TF; interaction shown by BiFC)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with FaMYB5 and FaLWD1/FaLWD1-like
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Addition of FaEGL3 caused the strongest enhancement of downstream gene expression among ternary components.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaEGL3 physically interacts with FaMYB5 and WD40 partners. 2. Co-expression with FaMYB5 markedly strengthens PA accumulation and downstream gene activation. 3. It defines a new MBW complex distinct from previously described strawberry MBWs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking partner with FaMYB5 to amplify flavonoid accumulation, especially PAs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not characterized alone for yield or growth effects.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid pathway module controlled by FaMYB5-FaEGL3 MBW
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances expression of flavonoid biosynthetic and transport genes when combined with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports FaMYB5/FaEGL3-mediated anthocyanin and PA accumulation as a WD40 component of the MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-overexpression in fruits; HPLC; DMACA assay; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: WD40 partner that strengthens FaMYB5-centered MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous to AN11-like WD40 component rather than the commonly emphasized TTG1 in prior strawberry studies.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaLWD1 interacts with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3. 2. In ternary co-expression, it enhances PA accumulation and target-gene expression. 3. It helps define a new strawberry MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential cofactor for pathway engineering to maximize FaMYB5-mediated flavonoid enhancement.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Flavonoid pathway module controlled by FaMYB5-FaEGL3 MBW
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances expression of flavonoid biosynthetic and transport genes when combined with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports FaMYB5/FaEGL3-mediated anthocyanin and PA accumulation as an alternative WD40 component of the MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; co-overexpression in fruits; HPLC; DMACA assay; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Alternative WD40 partner that strengthens FaMYB5-centered MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally similar to FaLWD1 in this study; changing pattern similar in co-overexpression assays.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaLWD1-like interacts with FaMYB5 and FaEGL3. 2. It supports ternary MBW-mediated activation of flavonoid genes. 3. It behaves similarly to FaLWD1 in enhancing PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative WD40 partner for stacking with FaMYB5/FaEGL3 in flavonoid engineering.
